column2:
65
祖母は、携帯電話の使い方を教えても、覚えるそばから忘れてしまう。
（＝覚えてもすぐに）

小さい子どもは、片付けているそばから部屋を散らかす。
（＝片付けてしびあともすぐに）
column3:
◆Meaning: No sooner does one action happen than another action follows and cancels, reverses or spoils its result.
◆Nuance: It commonly describes the same frustrating situation happening repeatedly. It is therefore different from ～たとたん, which usually focuses on one particular immediate occurrence.
◆Vるそばから: Presents the first action as something that repeatedly happens, with the second action following almost immediately each time.
◆Vたそばから: Emphasizes that the first action has just been completed when the next action occurs.
◆Vているそばから: The second action begins while the first action is still in progress or while its result is still being produced.
◆Memory: Imagine repairing something from one side while it is already being broken again from right beside you. そば suggests that the next event is extremely close to the first one.

Vるそばから
Vたそばから
Vているそばから
◆動作の繰り返しに多い

column2:
181
あの人は社長として、無責任極まりない。
（＝これ以上ないほど無責任だ）

あの発言は一国の首相として、軽率なこと極まりない。
（＝これ以上ないほど軽率だ）
column3:
◆Core meaning: Extremely; to the highest conceivable degree. The speaker judges that the quality has reached an extreme.
◆Nuance: This is a strong, formal expression frequently used in written criticism, speeches and serious commentary. It can also describe extremely positive qualities, as in 光栄極まりない. Although it contains ない, the meaning is not negative: it intensifies the preceding quality.
◆na＋極まりない: The stem of a na-adjective attaches directly to 極まりない. This is the shorter and more commonly used construction, as in 無責任極まりない.
◆naなこと＋極まりない: Adds こと after the adjective. It has the same basic meaning but sounds more elaborate, literary and emphatic, as in 軽率なこと極まりない.
◆Aいこと＋極まりない: Used with an i-adjective followed by こと. This also has a formal and strongly evaluative tone.
◆極まる and 極まりない: Both can mean that a quality reaches the highest degree. 極まりない is generally more frequent as a fixed evaluative expression.
◆Memory: 極 means an extreme point. 極まりない literally suggests that there is no limit to how extreme the quality is.

na（なこと）
Aいこと
　極まりない
◆「極まる」と同じ意味
「極まりない」のほうがよく使われる

column2:
160
最近、息子は私の言うことなど、聞きやしない。
（＝全然聞かない）

ドッグフードを変えたら、おいしくないのか、食べやしない。
（＝全然食べない）

A「ああ、スカートにシミがついちゃった……。」
B「大丈夫だ。誰もわかりやしないよ。」
（＝全然わからない）
column3:
◆Meaning: Does not do something at all; there is no chance that someone will do or notice it.
◆Nuance: A forceful conversational version of ～はしない. It emphasizes complete negation and is more emotional than an ordinary negative verb.
◆Critical use: It can express irritation, complaint or criticism, as in 聞きやしない and 食べやしない.
◆Reassuring use: It can also strongly reassure someone that an unwanted event will not happen, as in 誰もわかりやしないよ.
◆Form: Attach やしない to the verb’s ます-stem. 聞きます becomes 聞きやしない, and 食べます becomes 食べやしない.
◆Register: Mainly spoken and relatively rough. It is unsuitable for polite or neutral formal communication.
◆Memory: Replace the は of ～はしない with the stronger spoken sound や: 聞きはしない becomes 聞きやしない.

V<ｓ>ますやしない
Vますやしない
◆「～はしない」の口語形

column2:
3
あなたに感謝こそすれ、恨んでなどいません。
(=感謝はしているけれど、絶対に)

白髪は普通増えこそすれ、減ることはない。
(=増えろことはあるが、絶対に)
column3:
◆Meaning: The first statement is certainly true or possible, whereas the contrasting statement that follows is absolutely not true or possible.
◆Nuance: A formal and literary contrast. こそ strongly affirms the first possibility, and the following clause normally contains a strong negative expression such as ～ない, ～ことはない or ～わけがない.
◆Vます-stem＋こそすれ: Strongly affirms the first action before rejecting its opposite. 感謝こそすれ means that gratitude is certainly felt, while resentment is absolutely denied.
◆N＋こそすれ: Strongly presents the noun as the applicable description or possibility before denying a contrasting description. This nominal use is also formal and limited mainly to written or rhetorical language.
◆Contrast: It is stronger and more formal than ～ことはあっても. The speaker is not merely comparing two possibilities but emphatically excluding the second one.
◆Memory: こそ shines a spotlight on the first item. Everything after すれ is contrasted with it and strongly rejected.

Vますこそすれ
Nこそすれ
後文が[ぜったい~ではない]ということを強調

column2:
46
この不景気はいつまで続くのやら。
（＝いつまで続くのかわからない）

言葉も話せないのに、来年１年間オーストラリアに行くことにした。どうなることやら。
（＝どうなるだろう）

奥さんと病気で亡くした彼に、どう声をかけていいものやら。
（＝どう声をかけたらいいのかわからない）
column3:
◆Shared meaning: These expressions present an unresolved question. The speaker wonders about something without expecting an immediate answer.
◆Shared nuance: They often sound like thoughts spoken aloud and can convey anxiety, helplessness, doubt or bewilderment. They are usually used with an interrogative such as いつ, どこ, 何 or どう.
◆～のやら: Expresses general uncertainty about a fact, reason, duration or situation. この不景気はいつまで続くのやら means that the speaker has no idea how long it will continue.
◆Nuance of ～のやら: Relatively neutral among the three, although the context may add worry or resignation. It is close to ～のだろうか or ～のかわからない.
◆～ことやら: Commonly expresses uncertainty or worry about how an event or situation will eventually turn out. どうなることやら focuses on the unknown future result.
◆Nuance of ～ことやら: Often carries emotional concern, apprehension or resignation. The speaker imagines the whole future situation but cannot predict its outcome.
◆～ものやら: Commonly expresses uncertainty about what action to take, what choice to make or how to deal with a difficult situation.
◆Nuance of ～ものやら: Often appears with forms such as どう～ていいものやら and emphasizes being at a loss. It is especially suitable when the speaker cannot determine an appropriate response.
◆Memory: のやら wonders about what is true, ことやら wonders how the situation will turn out, and ものやら wonders what one should do.

疑問詞
V/A（普）/Aい/na -> のやら/ものやら/ことやら

column2:
137
彼とは結婚を前提として交際しています。
（＝結婚するということを考えて）

事故対策は、事故が起こることを前提に練られている。
（＝事故が起こるということを考えて）
column3:
◆Core meaning: Treating something as an assumption, condition or basis on which a plan, action, discussion or relationship is built.
◆Nuance: The premise is accepted for the purpose of planning or acting, but it is not necessarily certain or desirable. For example, accident prevention assumes the possibility of an accident without suggesting that an accident is wanted.
◆Nを前提として: Formal and explicit. It emphasizes that N is being established or treated as the premise for the following action.
◆Nを前提にして: Has essentially the same meaning as を前提として but is somewhat more natural in ordinary speech and writing.
◆Nを前提に: A shortened adverbial form of を前提にして. It is concise and frequently used before a verb describing planning, consideration or preparation.
◆N前提で: A compact and comparatively conversational form meaning on the assumption of N or with N understood as the condition.
◆Contrast with ～ために: ～を前提に states the assumption on which an action is based; it does not state the purpose of that action.
◆Memory: 前 means before, and 提 suggests placing something forward. The premise is placed first, and everything else is constructed on top of it.

Nを前提として
Nを前提に(して)
N前提で

column2:
112
親の期待に応えるべく、努力して医者の道に進んだ。
（＝親の期待に応えようとして）

彼はよい席を手に入れるべく、２時間も前から並んでいた。
（＝手に入れようとして）
column3:
◆Meaning: In order to accomplish something; with the firm intention of achieving a particular goal.
◆Nuance: A formal and literary expression corresponding to ～ために or ～ようとして. It often suggests deliberate effort, determination and purposeful action.
◆Subject relationship: The person who intends to achieve the goal is normally also the person who performs the action in the following clause.
◆Vる＋べく: Attaches to the dictionary form of a volitional verb. The preceding action expresses the purpose, and the following clause describes the effort made toward it.
◆するべく: The regular form created by attaching べく to する. It is formal but structurally transparent.
◆すべく: A shortened classical form of するべく. It has the same meaning but sounds more concise, literary and formal.
◆Restriction: Because it expresses intention or purpose, it is generally unnatural with events that cannot be intentionally controlled.
◆Memory: べく is related to the classical べし, which expresses what should be done. The following action is performed to make that intended goal happen.

Vるべく
！ する→するべく／すべく
◆硬い表現

column2:
151
彼は金持ちぶっているが、本当は借金がたくさんある。
（＝金持ちのふりをしている）

彼女はお酒に酔うと、かわい子ぶった口のきき方をする。
（＝意識してかわいく見せようとした）

息子は悪ぶっているが、実は気の弱い優しい子です。
（＝悪いように見せている）
column3:
◆Core meaning: To behave as though one possesses a certain quality, identity, status or attitude, usually in a deliberate and noticeable way.
◆Nuance: Often implies that the displayed image is exaggerated, artificial or different from the person’s true nature. It commonly carries criticism, mockery or affectionate teasing.
◆N＋ぶる: Means to act like or pretend to be the kind of person represented by the noun. 金持ちぶる means to behave as though one were rich.
◆na-adjective stem＋ぶる: Means to deliberately display the quality expressed by the adjective. It is especially common with qualities that can be performed as an attitude or image.
◆ぶる: The dictionary form, used when stating the behavior generally.
◆ぶって: The te-form, used to connect the performed attitude to another action or continuing state. 金持ちぶっている describes someone who is maintaining the act of being rich.
◆ぶった＋N: Modifies a noun and means an N that displays or is characterized by the assumed attitude. かわい子ぶった口のきき方 means a manner of speaking designed to appear cute.
◆Lexicalization: Some combinations, such as もったいぶる, have become established words and may have a more specialized meaning.
◆Memory: Imagine the person putting on the noun or quality like a costume and performing that role in front of others.

na
N
　ぶる
　ぶって
　ぶったN
れい　親切ぶる
　学者ぶる
　先輩ぶる

column2:
111
空の青と相まって、紅葉がより美しく見える。
（＝空の青色と一緒になって）

努力と運が相まって、合格することができた。
（＝努力と運が一緒になって）

この冷蔵庫はデザインのよさも相まってよく売れている。
（＝ほかの理由とデザインのよさが一緒になって）
column3:
◆Core meaning: Two or more factors combine, interact and jointly produce or strengthen a particular result.
◆Nuance: It expresses more than simple coexistence. The factors work together so that their combined effect is important to the outcome.
◆Nと相まって: Indicates that N combines with another explicitly stated or contextually understood factor. 空の青と相まって means that the autumn leaves and the blue sky enhance one another.
◆Two nouns joined by と: In 努力と運が相まって, both factors are explicitly named as equal contributors to the result.
◆Nも相まって: Presents N as an additional contributing factor alongside another reason or several reasons already mentioned or understood.
◆Nuance of も: The particle も means that N is not the sole cause. It joins the other factors and further strengthens the resulting effect.
◆Register: A formal expression common in written descriptions, reports, reviews and formal speech. In casual conversation, ～と一緒になって or ～もあって is more natural.
◆Memory: 相 conveys mutuality. Imagine several factors meeting each other and multiplying their effects rather than acting separately.

Nと相まって
Nも相まって
◆硬い表現

column2:
124
彼女は涙ながらに真実を訴えた。
（＝涙を流しながら）

コンピューターのおかげで、家にいながら(にして)世界の情報を知ることができる。
（＝家にいる状態で）

その店は、昔ながらの製法で豆腐を作っている。
（＝昔のままの）
column3:
◆Shared idea: Something happens while a particular state, condition or characteristic remains present and unchanged.
◆N＋ながらに: Indicates that an action is performed with the state or emotion expressed by the noun visibly present. 涙ながらに means while in tears or tearfully. This use is formal and largely limited to established combinations.
◆Vます-stem＋ながら: Indicates that someone remains in the state expressed by the verb while another action or situation becomes possible. 家にいながら means while remaining at home or without leaving home.
◆Vます-stem＋ながらにして: Has the same basic meaning but emphasizes the surprising fact that something can be accomplished without leaving or changing the original state. にして makes the expression more formal and emphatic.
◆ながらの＋N: Describes something that retains its traditional, original or familiar characteristics. 昔ながらの製法 means a production method preserved from the old days.
◆生まれながらにして: A fixed expression meaning from birth, innately or naturally possessing a quality without acquiring it later.
◆Usage note: These patterns are not completely productive. Many combinations, including 涙ながらに, 昔ながらの and 生まれながらにして, function as established expressions.
◆Memory: ながら preserves the original state. The next action happens while that state remains unchanged.

N
na
Aい
Vます
　ながらに
　ながらのN
慣用句　昔ながらの
　生まれながらにして
（＝生まれたときから）

column2:
32
雨が降ろうと降るまいと、試合は行われます。
（＝雨が降っても降らなくても、どの試合でも）

私がいようがいまいが関係なく、彼らはけんかを始めた。
（＝いてもいなくても）
column3:
◆Meaning: Whether something happens or does not happen, the result, decision or situation remains unchanged.
◆Nuance: The speaker presents two opposite possibilities and emphasizes that neither one affects the outcome. It often conveys determination, indifference or the irrelevance of the condition.
◆VようとVるまいと: Uses と to present the positive and negative alternatives. It means whether V or not V and is formal and emphatic.
◆VようがVるまいが: Uses が instead of と but has essentially the same meaning. It also emphasizes that the result is unaffected by either alternative.
◆Difference between と and が: The two forms are normally interchangeable. The choice usually reflects rhythm or style rather than a clear difference in meaning.
◆Vよう: The first verb takes its volitional form and represents the possibility that the action happens.
◆Vるまい: まい is a formal negative-volitional form meaning that something probably will not happen or will not be done. In this paired construction, it represents the negative alternative rather than the speaker's personal intention.
◆ru-verbs: Both Vるまい and the shorter Vまい may occur, such as 食べるまい and 食べまい.
◆u-verbs: The dictionary form is normally followed by まい, such as 行くまい and 降るまい.
◆Irregular verbs: する becomes するまい or すまい, and くる becomes くるまい or こまい.
◆Register: More formal and literary than ～ても～なくても or ～ようが～ようが, and often used in firm declarations.
◆Memory: Put the positive possibility on one side and まい on the other. Whichever side becomes true, the conclusion does not move.

VようとVるまいと
VようがVるまいが
(ru-V) Vるまい/Vまい
(u-V) Vるまい
する→するまい/すまい
くる→くるまい/こまい

column2:
157
今度こそ合格してみせるよ。
（＝合格することを約束する）

ピアノ、うまくなったね。今の曲、もう一回弾いてみせて。
（＝弾いて聞かせて）
column3:
◆Shared idea: The speaker performs an action so that its successful completion or result becomes clear to another person.
◆Meaning 1 — strong determination: Vてみせる expresses a firm promise or determination to accomplish something successfully. 今度こそ合格してみせる means I will definitely pass this time and prove that I can do it.
◆Nuance of determination: Stronger and more emotional than simply ～つもりだ. It often suggests overcoming difficulty, disproving doubt or demonstrating one's ability through the result.
◆Meaning 2 — demonstration: Vてみせる means to perform an action in front of someone so that the person can see, hear or understand how it is done.
◆Vてみせて: A request for someone to demonstrate the action. 弾いてみせて means play it and let me hear or see you do it.
◆Context distinction: When the action concerns a future goal, it usually expresses determination. When another person asks to observe the action, it means demonstration.
◆Contrast with Vてみる: Vてみる means to try doing something for oneself. Vてみせる means to do it successfully or perform it for someone else to perceive.
◆Memory: みせる literally means to show. You either show another person how an action is done or show everyone that you can achieve it.

Vてみせる

column2:
145
40歳にして(初めて)車の免許を取った。
（＝40歳のときに、初めて）

この改革は、あの政治家にして初めてできたことだ。
（＝あの政治家だから）
column3:
◆Shared idea: にして emphasizes a particular time, stage, person or status as exceptional or decisive.
◆Meaning 1 — at a particular stage: Nにして means at the age, time or stage of N. It often emphasizes that the event occurred surprisingly early, late or only after reaching that stage.
◆Nにして初めて: Means only at N or not until N. 40歳にして初めて emphasizes that the event happened for the first time only upon reaching the age of forty.
◆Nuance of time and age: More formal and emphatic than Nで or Nの時に. The speaker normally regards the timing as notable or unexpected.
◆Meaning 2 — only someone of that calibre: A person＋にして初めて means that only that particular person, or only someone possessing that person's exceptional ability or position, could accomplish the result.
◆Nuance of a person＋にして初めて: It praises or emphasizes the person's unique qualifications. あの政治家にして初めてできた means that the reform was possible precisely because it was that politician.
◆Role of 初めて: 初めて adds exclusivity. It indicates that the result becomes possible only at that stage or only through that person.
◆Register: Formal and common in written commentary, biographies and speeches.
◆Memory: にして places a spotlight on one decisive point. Only at that point, or only with that person, does the result become possible.

Nにして(初めて)
◆強調

column2:
156
就職難など、能力がある学生にとっては、恐れるに足りない。
（＝恐れる必要はない）

この証拠だけでは彼が無罪であることを証明するに足りない。
（＝証明するに十分でない）

取るに足りないうわさ
（＝取り上げるほどの価値がない）
column3:
◆Core idea: 足りる means to be sufficient or worthy. に足りない indicates that something does not reach the necessary level of importance, value or adequacy.
◆Meaning 1 — not worth doing: Vるに足りない means that something is too insignificant to justify the action. 恐れるに足りない means that it is not serious enough to be feared and therefore there is no need to fear it.
◆Meaning 2 — insufficient to accomplish something: Vるに足りない can mean that the available facts, ability or evidence are not sufficient to perform or establish the preceding action. 証明するに足りない means insufficient to prove.
◆How to distinguish the meanings: With emotional or evaluative verbs such as 恐れる, 論じる and 注目する, it often means not worth doing. With verbs expressing proof, qualification or fulfilment, it often means insufficient to do.
◆取るに足りない: A fixed expression meaning trivial, insignificant or not worth taking seriously. It commonly modifies nouns such as うわさ, 問題 and 人物.
◆恐るるに足らず: A literary fixed expression meaning there is no need to fear it. 恐るる is an older form of 恐れる, and 足らず is a formal literary equivalent of 足りない.
◆Contrast with に足りる and に足る: The affirmative forms mean sufficient to do or worthy of doing, as in 信頼するに足る人物.
◆Register: Formal and frequently used in written evaluations, arguments and commentary.
◆Memory: Imagine a scale measuring whether something has enough weight. に足りない means that it does not weigh enough to deserve the action or achieve the purpose.

Vるに足りない
Nするに足りない
慣用句　取るに足りない
　恐るるに足らず
（＝恐れるに足りない）

column2:
126
彼女は外見もさることながら性格もとてもいい。
（＝外見ももちろんいいが）

最近、忙しすぎて、疲労もさることながら、ストレスもピークに達している。
（＝もちろん疲労が激しいが）
column3:
◆Meaning: N₁ is certainly important or noteworthy, but N₂ is also noteworthy and is often the point that the speaker particularly wants to emphasize.
◆Nuance: The speaker does not dismiss N₁. N₁ is acknowledged as obvious or already significant before attention shifts to N₂.
◆N₁もさることながらN₂も: Means N₁ goes without saying, but N₂ also or not only N₁ but also N₂.
◆Focus: The main informational focus normally falls on N₂. Depending on context, N₂ may be equally important or even more remarkable than N₁.
◆Positive use: It can praise two qualities, as when both someone's appearance and personality are attractive.
◆Negative use: It can also emphasize two problems, as when both fatigue and stress have become severe.
◆さること: Here, さること has the sense of that being so or that going without saying. It does not refer to a distant object or person.
◆Register: Formal and more common in writing, prepared speech and careful commentary than in casual conversation.
◆Memory: First place N₁ safely to one side as something already acknowledged. Then ながら turns the listener's attention toward the additional point N₂.

N₁もさることながらN₂も

column2:
128
貧しさゆえに、彼は盗みを働いた。
（＝貧しいという理由で）

日本語を知らなかったがゆえに、誤解された。
（＝知らなかっただけの理由で）

有名人ゆえの悩みがある。
（＝有名人であるための）
column3:
◆Core meaning: Because of, due to or precisely as a consequence of the preceding fact or condition.
◆Nuance: A formal and literary expression of reason. It often introduces a serious, unfortunate, ironic or unavoidable consequence, although it can also be used for neutral or positive results.
◆～ゆえに: Directly presents the preceding fact as the cause or reason for the following result. 貧しさゆえに means because of poverty.
◆～がゆえに: Has the same basic meaning but が marks the preceding clause more explicitly as the decisive reason. It can sound more emphatic and literary, especially when the consequence is unfortunate or ironic.
◆～ゆえの＋N: Modifies a noun and means an N arising from or caused by the preceding state. 有名人ゆえの悩み means worries that exist because one is famous.
◆N and na-adjectives: The formal copula である is used instead of だ, as in 有名人であるがゆえに and 貧乏であったがゆえに. In some fixed or concise expressions, the noun may attach directly to ゆえ.
◆Verbs and i-adjectives: Attach in their plain forms, as in 知らなかったがゆえに and 貧しいがゆえに.
◆Contrast with から and ので: ゆえに is much more formal and weighty. It foregrounds the causal relationship rather than merely giving an everyday explanation.
◆Memory: ゆえ itself means reason or cause. Everything before ゆえ supplies the reason for what follows.

V/A/na/N(普)
！ Nだ／である
　naだ／である
　(が)ゆえ(に)
　(が)ゆえのN
れい　有名人である(が)ゆえの
　貧しい(が)ゆえに
　貧乏であった(が)ゆえに

column2:
162
不本意だがそうせざるを得ない。
（＝そうしなければならない）

今回の失敗は君の責任と言わざるを得ない。
（＝言わなければならない）
column3:
◆Meaning: To have no choice but to do something; to be unable to avoid doing it because of the circumstances.
◆Nuance: The action is normally contrary to the speaker's wishes, preferences or initial intentions. It expresses reluctant necessity rather than a freely chosen plan.
◆External pressure: The necessity usually arises from facts, rules, evidence or circumstances that leave no reasonable alternative.
◆Judgment use: With verbs such as 言う, 認める and 判断する, it means that the available facts force the speaker to reach a particular conclusion. 言わざるを得ない means one cannot avoid saying or concluding it.
◆Form: Replace the final ます of the verb's ます-form with ざるを得ない. 行きます becomes 行かざるを得ない, and 食べます becomes 食べざるを得ない.
◆する: The classical negative form of する is せざる, so しない becomes せざるを得ない.
◆得ない: In this expression, 得ない means cannot obtain or cannot have. The entire expression suggests that not doing the action is not an available option.
◆Contrast with なければならない: なければならない neutrally states an obligation. ざるを得ない emphasizes reluctance and the absence of any realistic alternative.
◆Register: Formal and common in serious speech, reports and writing. In casual conversation, ～しかない is more natural.
◆Memory: First form the literary negative ざる, then add を得ない. You cannot obtain the option of not doing it, so you have no choice but to act.

Vますざるを得ない
！ しない→せざる

column2:
2
苦労こそあれ、介護の仕事はやりがいがある。
(=苦労はあるけれど)

彼の日本語は小さい間違いこそあれ、ほとんど完璧だ。(=小さい間違いはあるけれど)
column3:
◆Meaning: Although the stated problem, weakness or condition certainly exists, the contrasting statement that follows is nevertheless true.
◆Nuance: こそ strongly acknowledges the first point instead of denying or minimizing it. The speaker then presents a more important positive judgment or contrasting conclusion.
◆N＋こそあれ: Means although there is N or N certainly exists. The noun commonly expresses a drawback, limitation or minor problem.
◆naでこそあれ: Used with a na-adjective or nominal description and means although it is certainly that way. It is formal and relatively uncommon in ordinary conversation.
◆こそあるが: A related form with the same concessive structure. It is somewhat more transparent in modern Japanese, while こそあれ sounds more compact, formal and literary.
◆Contrast with ～が and ～けれど: Ordinary concessive conjunctions simply connect contrasting facts. こそあれ deliberately emphasizes and concedes the first fact before shifting to the stronger conclusion.
◆Typical structure: A drawbackこそあれ, positive conclusion. For example, the work certainly involves hardship, but it is rewarding.
◆Memory: こそ places a spotlight on the weakness and openly admits it. あれ then turns toward the stronger point that follows.

Nこそ/naでこそ　-> あれ/あるが

column2:
185
良きにつけ悪しき良きにつけ、子どもは親の影響を受ける。
（＝良くても悪くても）
column3:
◆Meaning: Whether the situation is good or bad, the same general result or observation applies in either case.
◆Paired construction: AにつけBにつけ presents two contrasting alternatives and states that the following result holds regardless of which alternative occurs.
◆Nuance: The alternatives are often opposites representing the full range of possibilities. The expression is formal and rhetorical and emphasizes that there are no relevant exceptions.
◆良きにつけ悪しきにつけ: A fixed literary expression meaning for better or worse, whether positively or negatively, or in both good and bad respects.
◆良き and 悪しき: These are classical attributive forms corresponding to modern 良い and 悪い. Their use gives the expression a traditional and literary tone.
◆いいにつけ、悪いにつけ: A modernized version with the same basic meaning. It is easier to understand but still sounds more formal than 良くても悪くても.
◆General meaning of につけ: Outside this paired pattern, につけ can mean whenever or each time something happens. In the paired AにつけBにつけ construction, however, it means whether A or B.
◆Memory: Attach the same conclusion to both alternatives with につけ. Good or bad, the result remains connected to both sides.

Aいにつけ(Aいにつけ)
◆慣用的表現
OK　良きにつけ悪しきにつけ
　いいにつけ、悪いにつけ

column2:
125
自分の家は狭いながらも落ち着く。
（＝狭いけれど）

彼は学生の身分でありながら、高級車を乗り回している。
（＝学生という身分なのに）
column3:
◆Core meaning: Although the preceding fact or condition is true, the following result contrasts with what would normally be expected from it.
◆Concessive ながら: This use means although or despite being. It must be distinguished from the simultaneous-action use of Vます-stem＋ながら meaning while doing.
◆～ながら: Presents a contrast between two facts. 学生の身分でありながら means although he has the status of a student.
◆～ながらも: Has the same concessive meaning, but も makes the contrast more explicit and emphatic. 狭いながらも落ち着く stresses that the house is comfortable despite being small.
◆N and na-adjectives: Forms such as Nでありながら and naでありながら are common when stating a status or condition.
◆i-adjectives: Attach directly, as in 狭いながら and 狭いながらも.
◆Verbs: Attach to the ます-stem in formal constructions, although でありながら is particularly frequent when describing a state or position.
◆残念ながら: A fixed introductory expression meaning unfortunately or regrettably.
◆今さらながら: Means even now, although it may be rather late, or belatedly realizing something.
◆及ばずながら: A humble expression meaning although my ability or contribution is inadequate.
◆勝手ながら: A polite formula meaning although this is selfish or presumptuous of me. It commonly introduces a request, decision or announcement that may inconvenience someone.
◆Register: The productive concessive pattern is relatively formal. Several fixed expressions are common in polite announcements and business writing.
◆Memory: The first condition remains true, but ながら turns toward a result that goes against the expectation created by it. Adding も strengthens that unexpected contrast.

N
na
Aい
Vます
　ながら
　ながらも
慣用句　残念ながら
　今さらながら
　及ばずながら
　勝手ながら～

column2:
161
政治の世界には、知られざる真実がたくさんある。
（＝知られていない）

絶えざる不安に夜も眠れない。
（＝絶えない、ずっと続く）
column3:
◆Core meaning: ざる is a classical or literary equivalent of ない used before a noun. VざるN therefore means an N that is not V-ed or does not V.
◆Meaning 1 — simple negation: 知られざる真実 means truths that are not known, have not been revealed or remain hidden from the public.
◆Nuance of 知られざる: More dramatic and literary than 知られていない. It is frequently used in titles, journalism and descriptions intended to create mystery or importance.
◆Meaning 2 — continuing absence of an ending: 絶えざる不安 means anxiety that never ceases and continues constantly.
◆Nuance of 絶えざる: With verbs such as 絶える, 尽きる and 変わる, the negative form can describe an unbroken or continuous state, such as unceasing anxiety or unfailing support.
◆Form: Attach ざる to the verb form obtained by removing ます and changing the verb to its classical negative base. 知られます becomes 知られざる, and 絶えます becomes 絶えざる.
◆Irregular する: する becomes せざる, as in 予期せざる結果.
◆Register: Strongly formal, literary and often dramatic. In ordinary conversation, VないN or VていないN is more natural.
◆Contrast with ざるを得ない: In VざるN, ざる simply negates the verb and modifies a noun. In ざるを得ない, it forms part of the fixed expression meaning to have no choice but to do something.
◆Memory: Treat ざる as an old-fashioned ない placed directly before a noun. 知られざる真実 is simply 知られていない真実 in a more literary style.

VますざるN

column2:
155
彼は、信頼に足る人物だ。何でも相談するといい。
（＝十分信頼することのできる）

彼女は、その仕事を任せるに足る人です。
（＝十分任せることができる）
column3:
◆Core meaning: Sufficiently worthy, capable or reliable for the action expressed before に足る.
◆Vる＋に足る＋N: Describes a person or thing that has enough value, ability or credibility to justify the action. 任せるに足る人 means a person capable and reliable enough to be entrusted with the work.
◆Verbal noun＋に足る＋N: With verbal nouns such as 信頼, 尊敬, 報告 and 調査, する is normally omitted. 信頼に足る人物 means a person worthy of trust.
◆Nuance: This is an objective and formal evaluation. The speaker judges that the person, action or information meets a sufficiently high standard.
◆Register: Primarily used in formal writing, evaluations, reports and serious speech. In ordinary conversation, ～する価値がある or 十分～できる is more natural.
◆Contrast with ～に足りない: ～に足る means worthy or sufficient, whereas ～に足りない means insufficient, insignificant or not worth doing.
◆Memory: 足る means to be enough. The person or thing has enough quality to support the action placed before に足る.

Vるに足るN
Nするに足るN
れい　尊敬するに足る
　報告するに足る
　調査するに足る

column2:
130
川の水があふれんばかりになっている。
（＝今にもあふれそうな状態に）

彼女は泣き出さんばかりの顔をして、私に助けを求めてきた。
（＝今にも泣きだしそうな顔）
column3:
◆Core meaning: Appearing to be on the very verge of doing something, as though the action could happen at any moment.
◆Nuance: More vivid, dramatic and literary than ～そうだ. It often describes an appearance, emotion or quantity so intense that the action seems almost about to occur, even when it does not actually happen.
◆Vんばかりに: Functions adverbially and describes the manner or intensity of the following action or state. あふれんばかりになっている means that the water has risen to the point where it appears ready to overflow.
◆Vんばかりの＋N: Modifies a noun. 泣き出さんばかりの顔 means a facial expression suggesting that the person is about to burst into tears.
◆Form: Attach んばかり to the verb’s negative base. The ん has a classical meaning similar to ～ようとする or 今にも～しそうだ.
◆する: The negative base of する is せ, producing せんばかり.
◆Fixed expressions: 割れんばかりの拍手 describes applause so loud that the surroundings seem ready to break. あふれんばかりの笑顔 describes an exceptionally full and radiant smile. 胸がはりさけんばかりに describes an overwhelmingly intense emotion.
◆Memory: Imagine the action filling up to its absolute limit. Only one final step remains before it actually happens.

Vます→ん
！ しない→せん
　ばかりに
　ばかりのN
◆慣用句的表現
　割れんばかりの拍手
　あふれんばかりの笑顔
　胸がはりさけんばかりに

column2:
152
先輩の仕事ぶりを見ながら要領を覚えよう。
（＝仕事の様子）

社長の話しぶりでは、今年のボーナスはないようだ。
（＝話の様子では）

彼女、飲みっぷりがいいね。相当、酒に強いんだろう。
（＝飲む様子）
column3:
◆Core meaning: Describes the manner, appearance, performance or observable way in which someone does something.
◆N＋ぶり: Describes the observable state, performance or conduct associated with the noun. 仕事ぶり means the way someone works, including their ability, attitude and results. 活躍ぶり means the manner and extent of someone's achievements.
◆Vます-stem＋ぶり: Describes how an action is performed. 話しぶり means the way someone speaks, including tone, confidence and choice of words.
◆Inference use: ～ぶりでは or ～ぶりから can indicate that the speaker draws a conclusion from someone's observable behaviour. 社長の話しぶりでは means judging from the way the president spoke.
◆Vます-stem＋っぷり: A more conversational and emphatic variant. It often evaluates a vigorous, impressive or clearly noticeable manner of doing something, as in 飲みっぷり and 食べっぷり.
◆Nuance of っぷり: Frequently conveys admiration, surprise, criticism or amusement. 飲みっぷりがいい praises the person's bold or impressive way of drinking.
◆Usage restriction: っぷり is common in established combinations but cannot necessarily be attached naturally to every verb.
◆Different grammar: This use of ぶり is different from expressions such as ３年ぶり, where ぶり indicates the first occurrence after a period of time.
◆Memory: ぶり turns an action or role into a visible performance that can be watched and evaluated.

Nぶり
Vますぶり
Vますっぶり
れい　活躍ぶり
　読むっぷり
　食べっぷり
　走りっぷり

column2:
154
彼女のバイオリンは、あまりに下手で聞くにたえない。
（＝聞くことが我慢できないほどひどい）

昨日の映画は残酷で見るにたえなかった。
（＝見ることが我慢できないほどひどかった）
column3:
◆Core meaning: So terrible, painful, cruel or poor that one cannot bear to perform the action expressed before にたえない.
◆Meaning 1 — emotionally unbearable: Used when something is so cruel, tragic, embarrassing or unpleasant that watching, hearing or reading it causes emotional distress. 残酷で見るにたえない means too cruel to watch.
◆Meaning 2 — too poor to merit attention: Used when a performance, work or result is of such low quality that it is not fit to be watched, heard, read or appreciated. 下手で聞くにたえない means too badly performed to listen to.
◆Vる＋にたえない: The dictionary-form verb identifies the action that the observer cannot endure, such as 見る, 聞く or 読む.
◆Verbal noun＋にたえない: With verbal nouns, する is omitted, as in 鑑賞にたえない.
◆Nuance: This is a strong negative judgment. It does not merely mean that the speaker dislikes something; it means that the experience exceeds what can reasonably be endured.
◆Kanji: It may be written as ～に堪えない. 堪える means to endure or withstand.
◆Contrast with ～にたえる: The affirmative form means worthy of or capable of withstanding the action, as in 鑑賞にたえる作品.
◆Memory: The experience is placed on a scale of endurance, but it is so awful that the observer cannot 堪える it.

Vるにたえない
Nするにたえない
れい　鑑賞にたえない
　読むにたえない
◆「堪えない」とも書く

column2:
58
今度の演奏会は、東京を皮切りに、全国１０か所で行われる。
（＝東京を最初の場所として）

彼の店は大阪で成功したのを皮切りに、各地に次々と出店していずれも成功を収めた。
（＝成功したのがきっかとなって）
column3:
◆Shared idea: One place, event or achievement serves as the first step from which a series of similar events develops.
◆Meaning 1 — first item in a series: Nを皮切りに indicates that N is the first location, occasion or item in an organized sequence. 東京を皮切りに means beginning in Tokyo and then continuing through other locations.
◆Meaning 2 — initial event that starts a succession: A completed event can be nominalized with の and presented as the starting point for a chain of later developments. 大阪で成功したのを皮切りに means that the Osaka success was followed by successive openings and successes elsewhere.
◆Nuance: The expression normally requires more than one later event. It suggests that the initial event opens an expanding, repeated or organized series.
◆Nを皮切りにして: Explicitly presents N as the starting point. It has the same basic meaning as を皮切りに.
◆Nを皮切りとして: Slightly more formal and often used when describing schedules, campaigns, tours and organized developments.
◆Contrast with ～をきっかけに: ～をきっかけに identifies a cause or opportunity for a change. ～を皮切りに specifically emphasizes the first occurrence in a continuing series.
◆Memory: 皮切り originally evokes making the opening cut. The first event cuts open the way for everything that follows.

Nを皮切りに(して)
Nを皮切りとして

column2:
138
隣国はオリンピックを境に、急速に発展した。
（＝オリンピックの後から）

日本では就学年齢を４月２日を境にして決めている。
（＝４月２日で区切って）
column3:
◆Core meaning: Treating N as a boundary that clearly separates two periods, conditions, categories or groups.
◆Meaning 1 — turning point in time: Nを境に indicates that a significant change occurs before and after an event or date. オリンピックを境に means that the country's development changed noticeably from the Olympics onward.
◆Nuance of a turning point: The expression highlights a clear contrast between the situation before N and the situation after N, even when only the later condition is explicitly stated.
◆Meaning 2 — dividing line or cutoff: Nを境にして can indicate a precise point used to separate people, dates or categories into different groups. ４月２日を境にして means using April 2 as the dividing line.
◆を境に and を境にして: Their meanings are essentially the same. Adding して makes the act of treating N as the boundary slightly more explicit.
◆Contrast with ～を皮切りに: ～を境に emphasizes separation and change across a boundary. ～を皮切りに emphasizes the first event in a continuing series.
◆Register: Common in reports, historical descriptions, regulations and formal explanations.
◆Memory: 境 means a border. Imagine drawing a line through time or a group, with a different situation on each side.

Nを境に(して)

column2:
147
この間の彼女の態度は、思い出すだに腹が立つ。
（＝思い出すだけでも）

地下鉄に乗っているときに大地震が起きたらどうなるだろう。想像するだに恐ろしい。
（＝想像するだけでも）

そんなこと、夢(に)だにも思わない。
（＝夢でさえ）
column3:
◆Core idea: だに emphasizes the smallest possible degree: merely doing something is enough to produce a strong reaction, or something does not occur even at the most minimal level.
◆Vる＋だに: Means merely doing V or even just V-ing. 思い出すだに腹が立つ means that simply remembering the incident is enough to cause anger.
◆Nuance of Vるだに: Often used with mental or perceptual verbs such as 思う, 想像する, 聞く and 見る. The resulting emotion is usually intense, such as fear, anger, sadness or disgust.
◆N＋だに: Means even N or as much as N. It presents N as an extreme minimum that would normally be expected.
◆Nにだに and Nだにも: The particles に and も may appear with だに. も strengthens the sense of even, especially in a negative sentence.
◆Negative use: 夢にだにも思わない means not even to imagine it in one's dreams. When followed by a negative predicate, だに emphasizes complete absence.
◆Register: Strongly formal, literary and old-fashioned. It is often found in written descriptions and dramatic statements rather than ordinary conversation.
◆Contrast with ～だけでも and ～さえ: These express similar ideas in modern Japanese. だに sounds more solemn and emotionally charged.
◆Memory: Think of だに as reducing the action to its smallest possible amount. Even that tiny amount is enough to cause the reaction.

Vるだに
Nだに
◆硬い表現

column2:
75
大学生ともあろう者が、その漢字を読めないのは恥ずかしい。
（＝大学生という身分の者が）

一国の首相たる者が、このような発言をしてはいけない。
（＝首相の立場にある人が）
column3:
◆Shared idea: Both expressions emphasize the responsibilities and standards expected from a person or organization holding a particular status.
◆N₁ともあろうN₂: Means someone or something of N₁'s supposedly respectable status. It expresses surprise, disappointment or criticism because the actual conduct falls below what is expected.
◆Nuance of ともあろう: The speaker assumes that a university student, company president, teacher or respected organization should know or behave better. The contradiction between status and behaviour is central.
◆Typical nouns after ともあろう: Common following nouns include 人, 者 and 企業. The construction can refer to either individuals or organizations.
◆N₁たるN₂: Means a person who holds the position, role or identity of N₁. It emphasizes the duties, principles and conduct appropriate to that role.
◆Nuance of たる: More formal, solemn and normative than ともあろう. It often means that someone in that position ought to behave in a certain way.
◆Difference: ともあろう focuses on surprise that someone of such status behaved improperly. たる focuses on the responsibilities inherent in occupying the position.
◆Following judgment: Both are frequently followed by criticism, prohibition or a statement of obligation, although たる can also introduce praise or an ideal standard.
◆Memory: ともあろう means even someone of that status, expressing shocked expectations. たる means standing as that role, carrying all its responsibilities.

N1ともあろうN2
N1たるN2
◆批判が続くことが多い
れい　社長ともあろう人
A社ともあろう企業
教師たる者

column2:
93
かなりひどい虫歯だったが、抜かずにすんでよかった。
（＝抜く必要がなく終わって）

図書館で借りたので、本を買わずにすんだ。
（＝買う必要がなくて解決した）
column3:
◆Core meaning: A situation is successfully resolved without having to perform an action, or an undesirable action or result is avoided.
◆Nuance: Frequently conveys relief because something troublesome, costly, painful or unnecessary did not have to be done.
◆Vずにすむ: A relatively formal form meaning to manage without doing V or to avoid having to do V. 抜かずにすんだ means that extracting the tooth proved unnecessary.
◆Vないですむ: The common conversational equivalent of Vずにすむ. It has essentially the same meaning and is natural in everyday speech.
◆Vなくてすむ: Also means that doing V is unnecessary because the situation can be resolved another way. It often focuses on the resulting absence of necessity.
◆する: The ず-form of する is せず, producing せずにすむ.
◆Positive and negative outcomes: The construction often concerns avoiding an undesirable action, but it can neutrally indicate that something was unnecessary, as in 本を買わずにすんだ.
◆済む: The kanji 済む conveys the idea that a matter is settled, completed or resolved.
◆Contrast with Vない: A simple negative only states that an action did not happen. Vずにすむ emphasizes that the action was unnecessary or successfully avoided.
◆Memory: The problem reaches 済む, a settled state, while the unwanted action remains undone.

Vない→ずに
！ しない→せずに
Vないで
Vなくて
　すむ
◆「済む」とも書く

column2:
61
その男の人は部屋に入るや否や、いきなり大声で怒鳴り始めた。
（＝入るとほとんど同時に／入ったとたんに）

彼は、それを口に入れるや、吐き出した。
（＝入れるとほとんど同時に／入れたとたんに）

その政治家は、形勢が不利と見るや、態度を一変した。
（＝不利だとわかってすぐ/不利だとわかったとたんに）
column3:
◆Core meaning: The second action occurs immediately after, or almost simultaneously with, the first action.
◆Vる＋や否や: A formal literary expression meaning as soon as V or the instant V occurs. The first action is barely complete before the second begins.
◆Vる＋や: A shortened form of や否や with the same basic meaning. It is concise and strongly written in style.
◆Recognition use: With verbs such as 見る, 聞く and 知る, the expression can mean immediately upon recognizing or realizing something. 不利と見るや means as soon as he judged the situation to be disadvantageous.
◆Nuance: The second event is often sudden, striking, unexpected or decisive. The expression emphasizes speed rather than the speaker's emotional reaction.
◆Tense: Although the first event has already happened, the verb before や否や remains in the dictionary form.
◆Subject: The subjects of the two actions may be the same or different.
◆Register: Formal and common in written narration, news-style descriptions and dramatic storytelling. It is uncommon in casual conversation.
◆Contrast with ～たとたんに: Both indicate immediate succession. ～たとたんに often highlights an unexpected event from the speaker's viewpoint, while ～や否や gives a more objective, rapid and literary sequence.
◆Memory: 否 means no, but here や否や suggests there is not even enough time to ask whether the first action has finished before the second begins.

Vるや（否や）

column2:
38
日本人は、子どもといわず、大人といわず、マンガをよく読む。
（＝子どもも大人も、だれでも）

私は、牛肉といわず、豚肉といわず、肉は食べません。
（＝牛肉や豚肉だけでなく）

最近の若者は、食事中といわず、テレビを見ている間といわず、いつでもスマホを手にしている。
（＝食事中やテレビを見ている間だけでなく）
column3:
◆Meaning: Without distinguishing between N₁ and N₂; both categories and all comparable cases are included.
◆Paired construction: N₁といわずN₂といわず presents two representative categories, situations or extremes and states that the following description applies to both.
◆Broader implication: The meaning normally extends beyond the two explicitly named examples. 子どもといわず大人といわず implies people of every age, not only children and adults.
◆Category use: 牛肉といわず豚肉といわず indicates that the speaker makes no distinction between different kinds of meat and rejects the whole category.
◆Time and situation use: 食事中といわずテレビを見ている間といわず means in either of those situations and, by implication, at virtually all times.
◆Nuance: Stronger than simply saying N₁もN₂も because it explicitly removes the distinction between them and generalizes the statement across the whole range.
◆Register: Formal and rhetorical. It is common in written descriptions and emphatic generalizations.
◆Not quotation: The いう in this expression does not introduce a quotation. The phrase functions as a fixed pattern meaning without calling or distinguishing something as N.
◆Memory: The speaker refuses to label one case as N₁ and another as N₂. Once the labels are removed, the same conclusion covers everything.

N1といわずN2といわず
♦N1、N2の区別なく

column2:
163
電話で済むことだから、わざわざ行くには及ばない。
（＝わざわざ行く必要がない）

メールを使えばいい。郵便で送るには及ばない。
（＝郵便で送る必要がない）

もっと！若者は言うに及ばず、老若男女を問わず、彼のコンサートに行っている。
（＝若者については言うまでもなく）
column3:
◆Meaning 1 — Vるには及ばない: There is no need to perform the action because it is unnecessary, excessive or can be replaced by an easier alternative.
◆Nuance of には及ばない: More formal than ～なくてもいい or ～必要はない. It often tells someone that a special effort, concern or courtesy is unnecessary.
◆Vるに及ばない: The particle は may be omitted without substantially changing the meaning. Adding は can emphasize the specific action that is unnecessary.
◆Verbal noun＋には及ばない: With verbal nouns, する is omitted, as in 心配には及ばない, meaning there is no need to worry.
◆それには及ばない: A fixed response meaning that will not be necessary or there is no need to go that far.
◆Meaning 2 — 言うに及ばず: A fixed formal expression meaning needless to say, not to mention or of course.
◆Nuance of 言うに及ばず: The first category is presented as so obvious that mentioning it is unnecessary. The sentence then extends the statement to a broader or less obvious category.
◆Typical structure: N₁は言うに及ばず、N₂も means not only the obvious N₁ but also N₂. 若者は言うに及ばず、老若男女 means not only young people, but people of every age and gender.
◆Difference between the two uses: Vるには及ばない rejects the necessity of performing an action. 言うに及ばず treats one fact as too obvious to require mention and then adds a broader fact.
◆Register: Both are formal. In ordinary speech, ～必要はない and ～はもちろん are more natural.
◆Memory: 及ぶ means to reach. In には及ばない, the situation does not reach the level where the action is necessary. In 言うに及ばず, the fact is already obvious before words even need to reach it.

Vる
Nする
　に(は)及ばない
れい　心配には及ばない
　それには及ばない

column2:
158
歯医者「虫歯がありますよ。」
患者「そうですか。まだ痛くもなんともないんですが……。」
（＝全く痛くない）

彼のこと、好きでもなんでもないけれど、なぜか気にかかる。
（＝好きでも嫌いでもない）
column3:
◆Shared idea: Strongly denies that a quality, feeling or category applies at all.
◆Aくもなんともない: Means not A at all or not A in any way. 痛くもなんともない emphatically states that there is absolutely no pain.
◆Nuance of Aくもなんともない: Stronger than the ordinary negative Aくない. It often corrects another person's assumption or reassures someone that there is no problem.
◆Naでもなんでもない: Strongly denies that something possesses the stated na-adjective quality. It means it is not Na or anything of the sort.
◆Nでもなんでもない: Strongly denies that something belongs to the stated category or deserves that description. 苦労でもなんでもない means it is no hardship at all.
◆好きでもなんでもない: Strongly denies that 好き accurately describes the speaker's feelings. The context may suggest emotional neutrality, but the grammar itself does not necessarily assert that the speaker feels neither affection nor dislike.
◆Role of なんとも and なんでも: なんとも reinforces the negation of an adjective or sensation. なんでも rejects the entire description or category represented by a noun or na-adjective.
◆Register: Conversational and emotionally emphatic. The tone can be reassuring, defensive, dismissive or irritated depending on context.
◆Memory: First deny the stated quality, then use なんともない or なんでもない to erase every remaining possibility that it applies.

Aくもなんともない
Naでも
Nでも
　なんともない
◆強調
れい　ほしくもなんともない
　苦労でもなんでもない

column2:
119
現状に即して物事を考えよう。
（＝現状にちょうど合うように）

銀行も、時代に即した新しいサービスを提供しなければならない。
（＝時代にちょうど合った）
column3:
◆Core meaning: To base an action or judgment closely on reality, facts, circumstances, rules or the needs of the time.
◆Nuance: The action should accurately reflect and remain appropriate to the stated basis, rather than relying on abstract theory, outdated assumptions or personal preference.
◆Nに即してV: An adverbial form meaning to do V in accordance with N or while closely matching N. 現状に即して考える means to think based on conditions as they actually are.
◆N₁に即したN₂: An attributive form describing an N₂ that is adapted to, consistent with or appropriate for N₁. 時代に即したサービス means services suited to the current era.
◆Typical bases: Common nouns include 現状, 現実, 事実, 時代, 法律, 規則 and 方針.
◆Contrast with Nに基づいて: に基づいて emphasizes the foundation or source of a judgment. に即して additionally emphasizes close conformity and practical appropriateness to that basis.
◆Contrast with Nに沿って: に沿って means following a policy, route or guideline. に即して often suggests adapting accurately to concrete facts or circumstances.
◆Register: Formal and common in reports, policies, laws, business discussions and academic writing.
◆Memory: 即 means to come into close contact. The action stays closely attached to reality, facts or the stated standard.

Nに即してV
N₁に即したN₂
◆硬い表現
れい　現実に即して
　事実に即して
　法律に即して

column2:
144
息子の部屋は壁という壁に車のポスターが貼ってある。
（＝すべての壁に）

チラシをこの地域の家という家に配って歩いた。
（＝すべての家に）

今日という今日は、この仕事をやり遂げなければならない。
（＝今日は絶対に）
column3:
◆Shared idea: Repeating the same noun with という strongly singles it out and intensifies its scope or importance.
◆NというN — complete coverage: Means every possible N or all N without exception. 壁という壁 means every wall that could be used, and 家という家 means every single house in the area.
◆Nuance of complete coverage: The repetition creates an image of systematically including the entire category. It is stronger and more vivid than simply saying すべてのN.
◆Physical and countable nouns: This use is especially common with clearly identifiable things or places that can be exhaustively covered, searched, filled or affected.
◆今日という今日は: A fixed emphatic expression meaning today of all days, this very day or today without fail.
◆Nuance of 今日という今日は: It does not mean every day. It singles out the present day as decisive, often after repeated delay, frustration or failure.
◆Emotional force: Depending on context, the construction can convey determination, exasperation, urgency or amazement.
◆Contrast: In 壁という壁, repetition expands the scope to all walls. In 今日という今日は, repetition narrows the focus to one especially important day.
◆Memory: Say the noun twice to leave no ordinary interpretation possible: either every N is included, or this one N is treated as uniquely decisive.

NというN
◆強調
慣用句　今日という今日
（＝今日は絶対）

column2:
78
大変だ！女性が血まみれになって倒れている。
（＝血をいっぱい流して／血だらけになって）

彼は汗まみれになって、一生懸命サッカーの練習をしている。
（＝汗をいっぱいかいて／汗だらけになって）
column3:
◆Core meaning: Covered, soaked or surrounded extensively by the substance or undesirable condition expressed by N.
◆Physical use: Nまみれ describes a person or object whose surface is heavily covered with something such as blood, mud, dust, sweat or oil.
◆Nuance: The substance is normally dirty, unpleasant, sticky or excessive. 汗まみれ can nevertheless have a positive context when it shows intense effort.
◆Nまみれになる: Emphasizes entering or ending up in a state of being thoroughly covered by N.
◆Metaphorical use: It can describe being deeply burdened or surrounded by an undesirable abstract condition. 借金まみれ means overwhelmed by debts.
◆Contrast with Nだらけ: だらけ means that there are many instances of N throughout something, such as mistakes or holes. まみれ suggests that something is coated, soaked or immersed in N.
◆Contrast with Nずくめ: ずくめ means entirely characterized by one kind of thing and can be positive, neutral or negative. まみれ normally has an unpleasant or burdensome image.
◆Usage restriction: It is not normally used merely because there is a large quantity of something pleasant. The image of contact, coverage or entanglement is important.
◆Memory: Imagine N smeared across the entire surface so thoroughly that it is difficult to separate the person or object from it.

Nまみれ
◆汚いものがたくさん付いているという状態を表す
れい　泥まみれ
ほこりまみれ
借金まみれ

column2:
97
この分では、締め切りの期限に間に合いそうもない。
（＝間に合う可能性は低い）

できそうもないことを、簡単に引き受けるな。
（＝できる可能性の低い）
column3:
◆Core meaning: Based on the present evidence, it appears very unlikely that the action will happen or can be accomplished.
◆Vます-stem＋そうもない: Strongly expresses that there is little or no apparent possibility of V. 間に合いそうもない means it does not look as though finishing on time will be possible.
◆Nuance of も: The particle も strengthens the negative prediction. It often conveys pessimism, resignation or a firm judgment based on the current situation.
◆Vます-stem＋そうにない: Also means that V appears unlikely to happen. It is somewhat more objective and written in tone than そうもない.
◆そうにもない: A related form in which も adds further emphasis to そうにない, meaning that something shows virtually no sign of happening.
◆Attributive use: The complete form can modify a noun, as in できそうもないこと, meaning something that appears unlikely to be possible.
◆Evidence-based judgment: This pattern normally describes an appearance inferred from visible circumstances, progress or ability. It is not simply a statement that the speaker does not intend to act.
◆Contrast with Vないだろう: Vないだろう is a general prediction. Vそうもない emphasizes that the current evidence gives no encouraging sign that V will happen.
◆Memory: Ordinary そう points toward a likely event. Adding もない removes even that appearance of possibility.

Vます→そう
　もない
　にない

column2:
149
涼しくなって、ずいぶん秋めいてきましたね。
（＝秋らしくなって）

別れた恋人から、脅迫めいたメールが届いて、怖くなった。
（＝脅迫のように感じられる）
column3:
◆Core meaning: To take on the appearance, atmosphere or characteristics of N without necessarily becoming a clear or complete example of N.
◆Nめく: Functions as a verb meaning to become N-like, to show signs of N or to have an air of N.
◆Nめいて: The te-form of Nめく. It commonly describes a gradual development or connects the emerging N-like quality to another state. 秋めいてきた means that signs of autumn have gradually become noticeable.
◆N₁めいたN₂: Describes an N₂ that seems somewhat like N₁ or gives the impression of N₁. 脅迫めいたメール means an email with the tone or appearance of a threat, without directly declaring it to be one.
◆Nuance: The resemblance is indirect, suggestive or incomplete. It is useful when the speaker senses a quality but does not want to classify something definitively.
◆Common combinations: It often attaches to abstract nouns involving atmosphere or interpretation, such as 謎, 皮肉, 秘密, 脅迫 and 冗談.
◆Contrast with Nらしい: らしい means characteristic or typical of N. めく means that something begins to display or appears tinged with N-like qualities.
◆Contrast with Nっぽい: っぽい is conversational and can sound casual or critical. めく is more literary, subtle and descriptive.
◆Memory: めく adds a light layer of N over something. It looks or feels like N, but it has not necessarily become N completely.

Nめく
Nめいて
N₁めいたN₂
れい　謎めいた女性
　皮肉めいた言葉
　秘密めいた手紙

column2:
27
田中さんは私に会う
なり泣き出した。
（＝会うと同時に/会ったとたんに）

部長は電話を切るなり、部屋を出て行った。
（＝電話を切ったと同時に）
column3:
◆Meaning: As soon as the first action occurs, the second action follows immediately.
◆Nuance: The second action is usually a direct, rapid reaction by the same person and often feels sudden, striking or unexpected.
◆Vるなり: Although the first action is completed before the second, the verb before なり remains in the dictionary form.
◆Subject relationship: The subject of the first and second actions is normally the same. The construction follows that person's continuous sequence of behaviour.
◆Typical second actions: The following clause often describes a sudden movement, emotional reaction or deliberate response, such as crying, leaving, shouting or grabbing something.
◆Narrative viewpoint: It is mainly used to report an event that the speaker observed or learned about. It is generally not used to issue a request, command or invitation.
◆Register: Formal and literary, but common in written narration and descriptions of rapid actions.
◆Contrast with Vるや否や: Both mean as soon as. なり more strongly follows one person's immediate reaction, whereas や否や more objectively emphasizes the almost simultaneous timing of two events.
◆Different pattern: Vたなり is a separate construction meaning that a resulting state continues unchanged. It should not be confused with Vるなり here.
◆Memory: The subject completes the first action and, without pausing or changing direction, moves straight into the second.

Vるなり

column2:
85
彼女は、20万円からするバッグをいくつも持っている。
（＝20万円もする）

７キロからある道を歩いて帰った。
（＝７キロもある）

この店を改装するには500万円からの資金が必要である。
（＝500万円もの）
column3:
◆Shared meaning: Emphasizes that a numerical quantity is surprisingly or exceptionally large.
◆Number＋からする: Used mainly for prices, costs and monetary value. 20万円からするバッグ means a bag costing as much as 200,000 yen or more.
◆Nuance of からする: The stated figure is understood as a strikingly high approximate amount or lower estimate, not merely a neutral price.
◆Number＋からある: Used mainly for measurable quantities such as distance, length, weight, height or number. ７キロからある道 means a road extending for as much as seven kilometres.
◆Number＋からの＋N: Places the emphasized amount directly before a noun. 500万円からの資金 means funds amounting to as much as five million yen or more.
◆Difference among the forms: からする combines naturally with monetary cost, からある with physical or countable extent, and からの functions attributively before the noun being quantified.
◆Role of から: It suggests that the amount reaches at least the stated figure. The main purpose is emotional emphasis rather than exact mathematical precision.
◆Register: Common in written descriptions and emphatic speech, especially when the speaker wishes to underline the scale of something.
◆Contrast with ordinary numbers: 20万円する merely states the price. 20万円からする presents that price as notably high.
◆Memory: Imagine counting upward and already reaching the large number stated before から. The true amount may extend even further.

N
　からある
　からする
　からの
◆Nは数字で、その数量が多いということを表す

column2:
141
日本の朝食と言えば、ごはんにお味噌汁でしょう。
（＝ごはんとお味噌汁の組み合わせ）

私は、家ではたいていジーンズにTシャツを着ています。
（＝ジーンズとTシャツの組み合わせの服）
column3:
◆Meaning: Presents N₁ and N₂ as a characteristic, natural or familiar combination.
◆Nuance: The particle に does more than simply list two nouns. It suggests that N₂ is paired with, added to or coordinated with N₁ as a complete set.
◆Food combinations: ごはんにお味噌汁 presents rice and miso soup as a conventional pairing associated with a Japanese breakfast.
◆Clothing combinations: ジーンズにTシャツ describes an outfit created by combining jeans with a T-shirt.
◆Typical contexts: The pattern is common for meals, clothes, colours, objects used together and other recognizable combinations.
◆Implied appropriateness: Depending on context, it can suggest that the combination is standard, suitable, expected or personally preferred.
◆Contrast with N₁とN₂: と neutrally connects two nouns. に highlights how the second item accompanies or complements the first as a combination.
◆Compact style: This use often appears inside a larger sentence without requiring a verb such as 組み合わせる.
◆Memory: Treat に as placing N₂ onto or beside N₁ to complete a familiar set.

N₁にN₂

column2:
122
彼があなたのことを好きだということは、言わずともわかる。
（＝言わなくても）
column3:
◆Meaning: Even without performing the action, the following result is still true or can still be understood.
◆Nuance: The action is unnecessary because the result is obvious, naturally possible or already established.
◆Vずとも: A formal and literary equivalent of Vなくても. 言わずともわかる means that something can be understood even without being said.
◆Concessive force: Depending on context, it can also mean even if one does not V, emphasizing that the following result remains unchanged.
◆Formation: Replace ない with the classical negative form ず and add とも. 言わない becomes 言わずとも, and 行かない becomes 行かずとも.
◆する: The classical negative form of する is せず, producing せずとも.
◆Role of とも: とも has the concessive meaning even if. Combined with ず, it creates even if not or even without doing.
◆Register: More formal and rhetorical than ～なくても. It commonly appears in writing, speeches, sayings and serious statements.
◆Contrast with Vずに: Vずに means without doing V and directly connects to another action. Vずとも emphasizes that the following result holds even in the absence of V.
◆Memory: ず removes the action, while とも says even under that condition. The result survives even when the action does not happen.

Vない→ずとも
！ しない→せず

column2:
131
その子はうれしいとばかりに、飛び上がった。
（＝本当にうれしい様子で）

彼は試験中、あきらめたとばかりに鉛筆を投げ出した。
（＝あきらめた様子で）

彼女は「いやだ」と言わんばかりに、首を振った。
（＝「いやだ」と口では言わないが、そのような様子で）

ここぞとばかりに、……。
（＝今、この時を逃してはいけないという様子で）
column3:
◆Shared idea: A person's expression, attitude or action communicates a message so clearly that it is as though the person were saying it aloud.
◆Sentence＋とばかりに: Means acting as if to say the preceding sentence or showing through behaviour that the preceding feeling or judgment is true.
◆Nuance of とばかりに: The person does not necessarily speak. The observer interprets the person's movement, expression or attitude as conveying the unstated message.
◆Emotion or state＋とばかりに: In うれしいとばかりに, the action vividly displays happiness. The meaning is close to acting in a way that clearly shows one is delighted.
◆Completed judgment＋とばかりに: In あきらめたとばかりに, the behaviour creates the impression that the person has completely given up.
◆と言わんばかりに: An expanded and more vivid form meaning as if about to say or as if saying. 言わん is a classical form related to 言おう, not an ordinary negative form.
◆と言わんばかりに＋action: Describes a nonverbal action that strongly expresses the quoted message. 首を振る can communicate いやだ without the words actually being spoken.
◆Sentence＋とばかりの＋N: An attributive form modifying nouns such as 表情, 様子 and 態度. It means an expression, appearance or attitude that seems to say the sentence.
◆ここぞとばかりに: A fixed expression meaning to seize what one considers the perfect opportunity and act with particular energy, enthusiasm or determination.
◆Nuance of ここぞとばかりに: It often suggests that the person has been waiting for precisely this moment and does not intend to let the opportunity escape.
◆Memory: Imagine silent behaviour becoming a speech bubble. とばかりに supplies the message written inside that invisible bubble.

[文]と「（言わん）」ばかりに
[文]と「（言わん）」ばかりのN
◆言葉では言わないがそのような様子で
◆N＝表情、様子、態度など
慣用句　ここぞとばかりに

column2:
88
あなたならではの発想で、この企画を考えてください。
（＝あなた独自の）

これは当店ならではの特別価格です。
（＝当店だけの）

このような習慣があるのは、この地方ならではです。
（＝この地方だけ）
column3:
◆Core meaning: A distinctive quality, ability, product or phenomenon that is possible only because it comes from N.
◆Nならではの＋N: Modifies a noun and means unique to N, characteristic of N or possible only for N. あなたならではの発想 means an idea that could arise specifically from your individuality or experience.
◆Nならではだ: A predicate form meaning that something is uniquely characteristic of N. この地方ならではです means that the custom is found specifically because of the nature of that region.
◆Nuance: The expression emphasizes the special background, abilities, circumstances or character of N that make the result possible.
◆Positive tendency: It frequently highlights a special merit, strength or appealing feature, such as a unique price, handmade quality or professional insight. However, it can also describe distinctive problems or limitations.
◆Typical nouns: Common bases include people, professions, shops, regions, cultures, handmade products and artistic media.
◆Contrast with Nだけの: Nだけの simply indicates exclusivity or possession. Nならではの suggests that the quality naturally results from what makes N special.
◆Contrast with Nにしかない: にしかない states that something exists nowhere else. ならでは additionally evaluates it as characteristic or uniquely enabled by N.
◆Register: Natural in both speech and writing, especially in praise, advertising, reviews and descriptions of local characteristics.
◆Memory: Think of なら as if it is N. Only under that special condition does this distinctive quality appear.

Nならでは
◆より内容を表すことが多い
れい　手作りならでは
　映画ならでは
　田舎ならでは
　教師ならでは

column2:
183
こんなすばらしい賞をいただいて、光栄の至りです。
（＝これ以上ないほど光栄）

若気の至りで、せっかく入った大学をやめてしまった。
（＝若いころの勢いが行きすぎた結果）
column3:
◆Core idea: 至り means reaching an extreme point. However, Nの至り has two distinct uses depending on the noun.
◆Meaning 1 — the highest degree of a feeling: With nouns such as 光栄, 感激 and 恐縮, Nの至り means the utmost N or an overwhelming feeling of N.
◆Nuance of 光栄の至り: A very formal and humble expression used when receiving an honour, award, compliment or important opportunity. It emphasizes that the speaker could not feel more honoured.
◆Meaning 2 — the excessive result of a state: 若気の至り is a fixed expression meaning a reckless act committed because of youthful impulsiveness or immaturity.
◆Nuance of 若気の至り: It is often used retrospectively to admit or excuse a past mistake. The speaker recognizes that youthful energy or poor judgment went too far.
◆Usage restriction: Nの至り is not freely productive. It appears mainly in conventional combinations expressing intense emotion or in the fixed phrase 若気の至り. Expressions such as 疲労の至り are unnatural.
◆Memory: 至り is the point reached at the very end. 光栄 reaches the highest possible level, while 若気 reaches the point where youthful recklessness produces a mistake.

Nの至り
◆慣用句的に使う／気持ちを表す
れい　感激の至り
　若気の至り
ダメ　疲労の至り

column2:
123
英語の本をたくさん買ったが、ほとんど読まずじまいだ。
（＝読まないままだ）

５着も試着したが、どれも気に入らず、買わずじまいで店を出た。
（＝買おうと思っていたけれど結局は買わないで）
column3:
◆Meaning: Something that was intended, expected or possible ultimately never happened.
◆Nuance: The speaker looks back on the final outcome and often feels regret, disappointment or a sense of unfinished business.
◆Vずじまいだ: States that the situation ended without the action ever taking place. 読まずじまいだ means that the books remained unread in the end.
◆Vずじまいで: Connects the unrealized action to a following result. 買わずじまいで店を出た means that the person left the shop without ultimately buying anything.
◆Intention is common but not obligatory: The pattern often describes something the person planned or hoped to do, but it can also describe an opportunity that simply passed unused.
◆Potential-form use: 買えずじまい means that the person was unable to buy it in the end. This emphasizes inability rather than a simple decision not to act.
◆Formation: Replace ない with ずじまい. 行かない becomes 行かずじまい, and 会わない becomes 会わずじまい.
◆する: The classical negative form of する is せず, producing せずじまい.
◆Contrast with Vないまま: Vないまま only describes a continuing state without the action. Vずじまい emphasizes the final outcome: the opportunity ended and the action never happened.
◆Memory: じまい is related to しまい, the end. The matter reaches its end while the action remains undone.

Vない→ずじまい
！ しない→せず
れい　会わずじまい
　行かずじまい
　せずじまい
　買えずじまい
◆残念な気持ち

column2:
34
来るにせよ来ないにせよ、必ず連絡を入れてください。
（＝来る場合も来ない場合も）

熱が下がったにせよ、しばらく安静が必要です。
（＝熱が下がった場合でも）

事故で車は前のほうがめちゃくちゃになったが、何にしろ、誰もけがをしなくてよかった。
（＝どういう場合でも）
column3:
◆Shared idea: にせよ and にしろ temporarily accept a possibility, condition or alternative before stating a conclusion that remains valid.
◆AにせよBにせよ: Means whether A or B. Two alternatives are presented, and the following conclusion applies in either case.
◆AにしろBにしろ: Has the same basic meaning as AにせよBにせよ. にしろ is somewhat more conversational, while にせよ often sounds slightly more formal.
◆Nuance of paired alternatives: The two possibilities may be opposites, representative cases or the complete range of possible outcomes. The speaker emphasizes that the distinction does not affect the conclusion.
◆Single ～にせよ: Means even if, even assuming or although the preceding fact is true. 熱が下がったにせよ means even if the fever has gone down.
◆Nuance of the single form: The speaker provisionally accepts the preceding information but warns that the conclusion should not be changed too much because of it.
◆Single ～にしろ: Has the same concessive meaning as ～にせよ but can sound more direct or conversational.
◆何にせよ／何にしろ: Fixed expressions meaning in any case, whatever the circumstances or anyway. They shift attention away from details toward the speaker's main conclusion.
◆だれにせよ, いつにせよ and どこにせよ: Mean whoever, whenever and wherever respectively, emphasizing that the conclusion applies regardless of the answer.
◆Contrast with ～ても: ～ても is neutral and common. ～にせよ and ～にしろ sound more deliberate and argumentative, as though the speaker is considering possibilities before reaching a judgment.
◆Memory: にせよ and にしろ mean set it as that case for the moment. Even after accepting that case, the conclusion remains the same.

[aにせよbにせよ]
[aにしろbにしろ]
V/A/na/N（普）にせよ
na/N にしろ
OK 何にせよ/にしろ
だれにせよ/にしろ
いつにせよ/にしろ
どこにせよ/にしろ

column2:
86
試験では一秒たりとも時間をむだに使ってはいけません。
（＝たとえ一秒であっても）

その店は、一円たりともまけてくれませんよ。
（＝たとえ一円であっても）
column3:
◆Meaning: Not even the smallest amount or instance of N.
◆Nuance: The speaker chooses the minimum conceivable unit and emphatically states that even that tiny amount is not permitted, available or possible.
◆Nたりとも～ない: Normally appears with a negative predicate. 一秒たりとも無駄にしない means not to waste even one second.
◆Type of noun: N is usually a minimal unit of time, money, quantity, frequency or substance, such as 一秒, 一円, 一滴, 一粒 and 一度.
◆Emphatic force: Stronger and more formal than Nも～ない. It often expresses strict determination, complete refusal or absolute prohibition.
◆Positive predicates: The construction is normally associated with negation. A positive predicate would generally sound unnatural because the pattern emphasizes that not even the minimum is allowed or occurs.
◆Register: Formal and rhetorical. It appears frequently in rules, declarations, speeches and dramatic statements.
◆Memory: Take the smallest possible unit and place it under a magnifying glass. たりとも says that not even that single unit escapes the negative conclusion.

Nたりとも～ない
◆N＝最小の数量
れい　一日たりとも
　一滴たりとも
　一粒たりとも
　一瞬たりとも
　一度たりとも

column2:
99
20年ぶりの故郷の変わりように驚いた。
（＝変わったその様子に）

彼女の子どものかわいがりようは、少し異常なくらいだ。
（＝かわいがる様子は）

ものは考えようだ。
（＝考え方による）
column3:
◆Core idea: Vます-stem＋よう turns a verb into a noun describing how an action is performed, how extensively it occurs or the particular method used.
◆Meaning 1 — observable manner or degree: 変わりよう means the way or extent to which something has changed. It often focuses on a striking visible result.
◆Nuance of observable manner: The speaker evaluates the action from its appearance, intensity or remarkable degree. The expression frequently appears with surprise, criticism or admiration.
◆Meaning 2 — characteristic way of behaving: かわいがりよう means the manner or extent of showing affection. It describes how someone habitually or noticeably performs the action.
◆～ようといったらない: An emphatic related pattern meaning that the degree or manner is beyond description, often because it is extraordinary.
◆Meaning 3 — method or approach: In ものは考えようだ, よう means the way one chooses to think about or approach something.
◆Nuance of 考えよう: The result depends on one's perspective. The same situation may appear positive or negative depending on how it is interpreted.
◆Difference from volitional ～よう: This よう is a noun-forming suffix attached to the ます-stem. It is not the volitional ending found in 行こう or 食べよう.
◆Contrast with V方: V方 usually neutrally means how to do something or the method. Vよう often evaluates the actual manner, degree or style in which the action occurs.
◆Memory: よう means the visible shape or way of the action. It turns the verb into an observable pattern that can be judged.

Vます→よう
れい　～よう（とい）ったらない
慣用句　考えよう

column2:
142
考えに考えて進学を断念した。
（＝よく考えて）

不景気だというのに、高級時計が売れに売れているという。
（＝次々とよく売れている）

彼女は、嘘に嘘を重ねて男からお金を引き出していた。
（＝次々と嘘を言って）
column3:
◆Shared idea: Repeating the same verb or noun around に strongly emphasizes intensity, repetition, accumulation or thoroughness.
◆Vます-stem＋に＋Vて: Means to do V thoroughly, repeatedly or to an exceptional degree before reaching a result. 考えに考えて means after thinking and thinking very carefully.
◆Nuance of VにVて: The repeated verb highlights the process leading up to the result. It often suggests great effort, long duration or emotional intensity.
◆Vます-stem＋に＋Vた: The past form emphasizes that the action occurred to an extraordinary degree. It may be used when looking back on the intensity or frequency of the action.
◆Vます-stem＋に＋Vている: Can describe an action or state continuing at an exceptional level. 売れに売れている means selling extremely well or one after another.
◆Positive and negative uses: The pattern can emphasize both deliberate effort, as in 考えに考える, and an uncontrolled phenomenon, as in 売れに売れる.
◆NにNを重ねて: Means to accumulate one N after another or repeatedly add the same thing. 嘘に嘘を重ねて means piling lie upon lie.
◆Nuance of NにNを重ねて: It often suggests a long or escalating accumulation. The context may be positive, as in 研究に研究を重ねる, or negative, as in 嘘に嘘を重ねる.
◆Contrast with とても: とても merely increases degree. These repetition patterns vividly portray repeated actions or layers accumulating over time.
◆Memory: The same word appears twice because one action or item is placed directly on top of another, creating intensity through repetition.

VますにVて
VますにVた
◆強調
NにNを重ねて
れい　研究に研究を重ねて
　我慢に我慢を重ねて

column2:
92
このにおい、なんとかならないものか！
（＝どうにかしたい）

大事な時計が壊れてしまった。どうにか直せないものだろうか。
（＝どうにか直したい）
column3:
◆Meaning: Expresses a strong wish to find some way to make a difficult or undesirable situation change.
◆Nuance: The speaker is not simply asking whether something is possible. The question indirectly conveys frustration, hope, dissatisfaction or a request for a solution.
◆Vないものか: Means is there really no way to V? or can't something be done? It often sounds direct, emotional or impatient.
◆Vないものだろうか: Has the same basic meaning but sounds more reflective, cautious and indirect because of だろうか.
◆Potential negative＋ものか: Forms such as 直せないものか focus specifically on whether it might somehow be possible to perform the action.
◆なんとか／どうにか: These adverbs reinforce the desire to find any workable solution, even if it is difficult.
◆もう少し: With expressions such as もう少し安くならないものか, the speaker wishes for an improvement in degree rather than a complete change.
◆Not the rhetorical ～ものか: A separate pattern, Vるものか, can mean absolutely not or as if I would. Here, the negative verb before ものか expresses a wish for a solution and should not be confused with that rejection pattern.
◆Contrast with Vたい: Vたい directly states the speaker's desire to act. Vないものか frames the wish as a search for a possible solution, often when circumstances are difficult to control.
◆Memory: The speaker looks at the negative situation and asks whether there is not even one もの, one possible way, to change it.

（どうにか）
（なんとか）
（もう少し）
　Vないもの(だろう)か
　Vれないもの(だろう)か

column2:
26
できないならできないなりに、無理をしないで少しずつやりましょう。
（＝できないなりにできる範囲で）

貧しいなら貧しいなりの生活をしなければならない。
（＝貧しいならできる範囲の）

結婚式に出席する
なら
、それ
なりの
服装をするべきだ。
（＝それにふさわしい）

あなたが会社を辞めた理由は私なり
に理解しています。
（＝私が考える範囲で）
column3:
◆Core idea: なりに and なりの indicate a level, method or form appropriate to someone's condition, ability, position or circumstances.
◆AならAなりに: Means in a way appropriate to being A or within the limits of that condition. できないならできないなりに means doing what is possible despite one's limitations.
◆Nuance of repeated form: Repeating the verb, adjective or noun acknowledges an imperfect or restricted condition without treating it as an excuse to do nothing.
◆AならAなりの＋N: Describes an N suited to or naturally resulting from condition A. 貧しいなら貧しいなりの生活 means a lifestyle appropriate to limited financial circumstances.
◆それなりに: Means in a manner appropriate to the situation, to a reasonable extent or in its own way.
◆Nuance of それなり: Depending on context, it can be positive, neutral or mildly limiting. それなりにできた may mean it was done reasonably well, though not exceptionally.
◆それなりの＋N: Means a suitable, corresponding or reasonably proper N. それなりの服装 means clothing appropriate to the occasion.
◆Person＋なりに: Means according to that person's own understanding, ability, perspective or method. 私なりに理解しています means I understand it in my own way or to the extent that I can.
◆Person＋なりの＋N: Describes something distinctive to that person's perspective or abilities, such as 私なりの考え.
◆Nuance: The construction often acknowledges limitations while still valuing an effort, interpretation or solution that fits those limitations.
◆Contrast with Nらしい: Nらしい means characteristic of N. Nなりに emphasizes working within N's actual circumstances or capacity.
◆Memory: なり creates a shape fitted to the person or situation. The action does not have to be ideal; it should fit the available conditions.

(Vるなら)Vる/(Aいなら)Aい/(naなら)na/(Nなら)N->なりに/なりのN

column2:
83
この状況にあって、利益を伸ばすのは困難だ。
（＝この状況において）

父は大学教授だが、家庭にあってはふつうの父親です。
（＝家庭においては）
column3:
◆Core meaning: In, under or within a particular situation, environment, organization or sphere.
◆Nにあって: Indicates the circumstances or setting in which the following situation exists. この状況にあって means under these particular circumstances.
◆Nuance of Nにあって: More formal and weighty than Nで or Nにおいて. It often emphasizes that the situation is difficult, exceptional or historically significant.
◆Nにあっては: Adds は to contrast one sphere or condition with another. 家庭にあっては means within the family context, in contrast with the person's professional role.
◆Contrastive use: The same person or thing may behave differently depending on the sphere. A university professor may still be an ordinary father at home.
◆Typical nouns: Common with 状況, 環境, 時代, 組織, 社会, 家庭 and 逆境.
◆Positive and negative settings: It can describe both adverse circumstances and privileged or exceptional positions.
◆Contrast with Nにおいて: Both mean in or at. にあって more strongly suggests existing within and being affected by the stated circumstances.
◆Register: Formal and common in essays, reports, speeches, historical descriptions and serious commentary.
◆Memory: あって comes from ある. The person or event exists inside the stated situation, and the following judgment is made from within it.

Nにあって(は)

column2:
109
君は見かけによらずよく食べるね。
（＝見た目には関係なく）

職種によらず、賃金によらず、仕事があれば何でもやります。
（＝職種や賃金に関係なく）

この学習書は、レベルによらず、役に立つので買ったほうがいいでしょう。
（＝レベルには関係なく）
column3:
◆Core idea: The conclusion does not depend on, follow from or vary according to N.
◆Meaning 1 — contrary to what N suggests: 見かけによらず means contrary to appearances or despite what one's appearance suggests.
◆Nuance of 見かけによらず: The actual quality or behaviour is unexpected when compared with the impression created by the person's appearance.
◆Meaning 2 — regardless of N: With categories, standards or conditions, Nによらず means irrespective of N or without distinction based on N.
◆Category use: 職種によらず and 賃金によらず mean that neither job type nor salary affects the speaker's decision.
◆Range use: レベルによらず means that the conclusion applies at every level, regardless of whether the learner is a beginner or advanced.
◆Nuance: The pattern removes N as a relevant criterion. The same result applies across all categories or conditions.
◆Contrast with Nにかかわらず: Both mean regardless of N. にかかわらず is broadly applicable and neutral, while によらず often emphasizes that the result is not determined by or based on N.
◆Contrast with N次第で: N次第で means that the outcome depends on N. Nによらず states exactly the opposite: N does not determine the outcome.
◆Register: Formal and common in written explanations, conditions, policies and evaluations. 見かけによらず is also common in conversation.
◆Memory: よる means to depend on. よらず removes that dependence, so the conclusion stands independently of N.

Nによらず

column2:
186
周囲の反対をものともせずに、二人は結婚した。
（＝周囲が反対しているのを全く気にしないで）

消防士たちは、危険をものともせずに、火の中に飛び込んで行った。
（＝危険を全く恐れないで）
column3:
◆Meaning: To confront a serious obstacle, danger or opposition without being discouraged, frightened or defeated by it.
◆Nuance: The person recognizes that the difficulty exists but treats it as powerless to stop them. The expression praises courage, determination or resilience.
◆Nをものともせずに: Functions adverbially and describes performing the following action despite N. 周囲の反対をものともせずに means without allowing others' opposition to deter them.
◆Physical danger: With nouns such as 危険, 嵐 and 敵の攻撃, it emphasizes bravery in facing a threatening situation.
◆Social or psychological obstacles: It may also be used with criticism, opposition, disability or hardship when someone continues despite pressure or disadvantage.
◆Evaluation: Usually expresses admiration, although it can sometimes describe reckless behaviour if the context is critical.
◆Subject: Normally used for deliberate actions by people or organizations capable of resisting the obstacle.
◆Contrast with Nにもかかわらず: にもかかわらず neutrally means despite N. をものともせずに adds the stronger image that the subject actively refuses to be defeated or intimidated by N.
◆Register: Formal and common in news reports, biographies, speeches and dramatic descriptions.
◆Memory: The obstacle is treated as though it were not even a もの worth fearing. The subject moves forward without giving it power.

Nをものともせずに
